raw_variable_names: METADATA; Group; V6; OUTCOMES1; V8; V10; V11; V12; V13; V15
model_formula: MANOVA cbind(effective, well_liked, clear_expectations_reversed, helpful, challenging_content, learning_environment, prepared_reversed) ~ participant_status * rating_magnitude * quizzing_comment, with sum contrasts and Type-III Pillai tests
contrast_direction: main effect of rating magnitude: high ratings versus low ratings
extraction_note: Experiment 1 only. The original outcome columns were used directly: OUTCOMES1, V8, V10, V11, V12, V13, V15. This avoids the partially missing manually derived variables helpful/challenging in the edited SAV. Complete-case N = 213; students = 117; faculty = 96; high-rating n = 106; low-rating n = 107. Type-III Pillai trace = 0.30958607, F = 12.747555, df = 7, 199, p = 1.7157317e-13.

raw_variable_names: METADATA; Group; V6; OUTCOMES1; V8; V10; V11; V12; V13; V15
model_formula: MANOVA cbind(effective, well_liked, clear_expectations_reversed, helpful, challenging_content, learning_environment, prepared_reversed) ~ participant_status * rating_magnitude * quizzing_comment, with sum contrasts and Type-III Pillai tests
contrast_direction: main effect of quizzing comments: comments mention daily quizzes versus comments mention no quizzes
extraction_note: Experiment 1 only. The original outcome columns were used directly: OUTCOMES1, V8, V10, V11, V12, V13, V15. This is the article's MANOVA effect for whether comments mentioned daily quizzes. Complete-case N = 213; quizzes n = 109; no-quizzes n = 104. Type-III Pillai trace = 0.022359476, F = 0.65018577, df = 7, 199, p = 0.71393359.
